vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1629: Substrate: concurrent 'pan start' invocations fail — bd list lock contention spawns only some agents

plan.title: Substrate: concurrent 'pan start' invocations fail — bd list lock contention spawns only some agents
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Firing multiple `pan start <id> --host --yes` in parallel (the Flywheel's core job) causes some to fail at the beads-resolution step. `pan start` runs `bd list --json -l pan-<id> --status all --limit 0` to resolve the task breakdown; under concurrent invocation that command fails on Dolt's file lock, and `pan start` then aborts with a MISLEADING 'No beads tasks found … Planning must create a task breakdown' message even though the beads exist (observed RUN-14: PAN-1613/1614/1617 failed, PAN-1615/1616 succeeded; staggering 12s apart fixed it). Root cause: the existing `withBdMutexPromise` is an IN-PROCESS mutex that serializes bd calls within one Node process but does nothing across the separate child processes the Flywheel spawns. The JSONL fallbacks (countBeadsTasks, queryBeadsForIssuePromise) failed to mask it because the committed worktree `.beads/issues.jsonl` snapshot is stale relative to live Dolt (commit 0e64035d2). A related symptom: `pan start --host --yes` does not return promptly under contention because its bd calls block on the Dolt lock.
narratives.Proposal: Make the `pan start` beads path safe under concurrent invocation by (1) a CROSS-PROCESS advisory lock keyed on the shared Dolt DB, mirroring src/lib/restart-lock.ts (no new npm dep), serializing bd access across separate `pan start` processes; (2) a bounded retry-with-backoff on transient/lock errors as the in-flight safety net; (3) routing the start-path gate query, the create-beads/dedup path, and the dashboard's beads-query through that lock+retry so the live count is reliably obtained instead of falling back to a stale JSONL snapshot; (4) honest error classification so a transient lock failure is never reported as 'planning must create beads' and never triggers destructive vBRIEF re-materialization; (5) verifying the `--host --yes` slow-return note is resolved (bd-lock blocking bounded) or fixing/documenting it; (6) a concurrency regression test reproducing the original failure and proving N parallel operations all succeed.

autoDecisions.summary: Cross-process lock mirrors src/lib/restart-lock.ts (atomic O_EXCL lockfile, PID+ts+caller, stale-reclaim) rather than adding a proper-lockfile dependency.
autoDecisions.rationale: We already have a battle-tested cross-process lock template in the repo; reusing the pattern keeps the codebase consistent and avoids a new runtime dependency for a primitive we can author in ~80 lines with tests.

autoDecisions.summary: Lock keyed on the resolved SHARED Dolt `.beads` directory (under getPanopticonHome()/locks/), not per-workspace.
autoDecisions.rationale: All worktrees redirect to the main repo's single Dolt DB, so concurrent starts for different issues contend on the same DB; a per-workspace lock would not serialize them. Placing the lockfile under the Panopticon home keeps it out of git-tracked .beads.

autoDecisions.summary: Apply the cross-process lock surgically to the start path + create-beads; use retry-on-transient as the universal safety net; do NOT globally rewire withBdMutexPromise/every dashboard bd call.
autoDecisions.rationale: Surgical change (Karpathy); the dashboard's bd hot path is already serialized in-process by the existing mutex, and globally taking a filesystem lock on every dashboard bd call risks destabilizing it. The retry wrapper covers residual contention from non-locked bd callers.

autoDecisions.summary: Treat the `pan start --host --yes` slow-return note as in-scope: investigate + verify, fix or document.
autoDecisions.rationale: It directly breaks the Flywheel's sequential fallback; leaving a known related defect unverified would violate the 'Deliver Complete Features' rule. The most likely cause is bd calls blocking on the Dolt lock, which the lock+retry work bounds.

autoDecisions.summary: Stop relying on the stale JSONL snapshot as the correctness path; the gate must get the live count via lock+retry.
autoDecisions.rationale: Both existing JSONL fallbacks predate RUN-14 yet failed to mask the bug because the committed snapshot omits beads written to live Dolt after the snapshot (commit 0e64035d2). A stale/empty JSONL must never be reported as 'planning must create beads' when a transient bd error is the true cause.

items.title: Add a conservative transient/lock bd-error classifier

narrative.Action: Add a pure predicate `isTransientBdError(error): boolean` (e.g. in a new src/lib/bd-process-lock.ts or src/lib/bd-mutex.ts) that returns true ONLY for Dolt/beads lock-contention signatures observed when a concurrent process holds the DB lock — and false for everything else, especially 'genuine empty result' (which is exit 0 + `[]`, never an error). First confirm the real Dolt lock stderr text by running concurrent `bd list` against a throwaway temp DB, then match those signatures conservatively (lock held / database is locked / resource temporarily unavailable / could not acquire). Cover the error shapes execFile/execFileSync produce (error.stderr, error.message, error.code). Default to NON-transient when uncertain.

subItems.title: isTransientBdError returns true for confirmed Dolt lock-contention stderr signatures
isTransientBdError returns false for a successful-but-empty bd list result and for genuine fatal errors (corrupt db, command-not-found)
Unit tests cover execFile error shapes (stderr string, message, code) and the empty-result false case
The confirmed Dolt lock stderr text is documented in a code comment citing how it was reproduced

items.title: Add a cross-process bd lock + bounded-retry wrapper (mirroring restart-lock.ts)

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/bd-process-lock.ts providing `withBdProcessLock(caller, fn, opts?)` and a `runBdWithRetry` helper. The lock is an atomic O_EXCL lockfile under `getPanopticonHome()/locks/bd-<slug-of-resolved-shared-beads-dir>.lock`, mirroring src/lib/restart-lock.ts: holder = {pid, ts, caller}; stale-reclaim when holder PID is dead OR age > bound; bounded acquisition wait with backoff+jitter; ALWAYS release in finally. Key the lockfile on the RESOLVED shared Dolt `.beads` dir (resolve `.beads/redirect` → main repo) so all worktrees of one project share one lock. `runBdWithRetry` wraps a bd invocation: acquire the process lock, run, and on isTransientBdError (from the classifier bead) retry with bounded exponential backoff+jitter; surface a typed/contextful failure when retries are exhausted. Provide both a Promise API and an Effect variant consistent with restart-lock.ts. This is the concurrency foundation the wiring beads build on — get the lock KEY (shared DB, not workspace) and stale-reclaim right or the bug persists / deadlocks.

subItems.title: withBdProcessLock serializes across SEPARATE processes (two concurrent acquirers do not both hold it); proven by a test that forks/simulates two holders
Lockfile path is derived from the resolved shared Dolt `.beads` dir so two worktrees of the same project share one lock
A stale lock (dead PID or age beyond bound) is reclaimed; a live holder is respected; the lock is always released in finally
runBdWithRetry retries on isTransientBdError with bounded backoff and stops after the bound, surfacing a typed transient failure (tests use vi.useFakeTimers — no real backoff sleeps)
Acquisition wait is bounded so a wedged holder degrades to a typed transient failure rather than hanging forever

items.title: Route create-beads/dedup (`clearBeadsForIssue` → `bd list`) through the cross-process lock + retry

narrative.Action: In src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts, make the dedup path resilient: route `listBeadsForIssue` and `clearBeadsForIssue`'s bd invocations through runBdWithRetry, and make the whole `createBeadsFromVBriefPromise` critical section additionally hold the cross-process lock (compose with the existing in-process withBdMutexPromise — do not replace it). The 'dedup failed: list failed:' abort (beads.ts:254) must no longer fire on transient lock contention; it should only fire on a genuine, retries-exhausted failure. Keep the existing bd-ping/doctor recovery and the create logic intact.

subItems.title: listBeadsForIssue/clearBeadsForIssue retry on transient lock errors instead of immediately returning 'list failed'
createBeadsFromVBrief holds the cross-process lock for its bd-touching section, composed with (not replacing) the existing in-process mutex
Existing src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts stays green (dedup-failure-on-genuine-error cases still abort)

items.title: Route the `pan start` beads-gate query and `beads-query.ts` through lock + retry (get the LIVE count, not stale JSONL)

narrative.Action: Make the beads-resolution gate reliably reflect live Dolt under contention. In src/cli/commands/start.ts: the gate (`hasBeadsTasks`/`countBeadsTasks` at lines 541/575, used only within start.ts) and `validateBeadsMatchPlan` must obtain the live count via the lock+retry helper before falling back to the JSONL snapshot — so a transient `bd list` failure retries and succeeds instead of spuriously returning 0 and triggering recovery. Prefer a bounded retry around the existing call (sync retry loop) or an async start-path helper; enumerate callers (all in start.ts) before changing signatures. In src/lib/beads-query.ts, wrap `queryBeadsForIssuePromise`'s `bd list` in retry too (the dashboard start-agent path). The JSONL fallback remains only as a genuine last resort.

subItems.title: Under simulated lock contention, the start gate retries `bd list` and returns the true live count (>0 when beads exist) instead of falling back to a stale/empty JSONL
queryBeadsForIssuePromise in beads-query.ts retries on transient lock errors before the JSONL fallback
No signature change leaks beyond start.ts callers; existing start.ts tests stay green

items.title: Never report 'planning must create beads' on a transient bd failure; emit an honest locked-retry message

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/start.ts, classify the recovery/gate failure (via isTransientBdError). When the underlying failure is a transient/lock error — or when the issue's beads are known to exist (live count seen previously OR non-empty JSONL) — do NOT print 'No beads tasks found … Planning must create a task breakdown' and do NOT trigger destructive vBRIEF re-materialization. Instead print an honest message (e.g. 'beads database was temporarily locked by a concurrent operation; retried N times — re-run `pan start <id>` shortly') and exit with a distinct, non-zero, retryable status code. The 'planning must create beads' guidance must only appear when beads are genuinely absent (live query succeeded and returned empty AND JSONL empty).

subItems.title: A transient/lock failure during gate or recovery prints a 'temporarily locked — retry' message, NOT 'Planning must create a task breakdown'
A transient read failure does NOT trigger destructive vBRIEF re-materialization (no bead deletion on a lock error)
'planning must create beads' guidance still appears only when beads are genuinely absent (live query returned empty AND JSONL empty)
The transient-failure exit code is distinct from the genuine-no-beads exit code so callers/Flywheel can distinguish retryable from re-plan

items.title: Verify `pan start --host --yes` returns promptly after spawn (the Related note)

narrative.Action: Confirm the secondary symptom is resolved. `pan start` returns after spawnAgent creates a detached tmux session (it does not attach), so the prior slow return was bd calls blocking on the Dolt lock. Establish that with the lock+retry work, each `pan start --host --yes` returns within a bounded time even under parallel launch (bd blocking is now bounded by the lock acquisition wait + retry bound, not the agent's lifetime). If a genuine separate attach/block bug is found, fix it; otherwise document that the prompt-return behavior is intended and the prior slowness was lock blocking. Provide a documented repro (or test) firing N parallel `--host --yes` and confirming all N return and all N agents spawn.

subItems.title: N parallel `pan start --host --yes` invocations each return within a bounded time and all N work agents are spawned
The cause of any residual delay is identified as bd-lock blocking (now bounded) — or a separate bug is fixed; the finding is documented

items.title: Regression test: N concurrent beads operations all succeed (reproduces the RUN-14 failure)

narrative.Action: Add a test that reproduces the original failure and proves the fix. Fire N (>=5) concurrent operations that exercise the start beads path (gate query + dedup/create) against a shared temp Dolt DB (or a deterministic mock injecting transient lock errors for the first attempts). Assert: no operation aborts with 'dedup failed: list failed', none emits the spurious 'No beads tasks found … Planning must create a task breakdown', and all N resolve beads successfully. Use vi.useFakeTimers for any backoff. This is the regression lock for the whole issue.

subItems.title: Test fires >=5 concurrent start-path beads operations and all succeed (no 'list failed', no spurious 'no beads')
Test injects transient lock errors on early attempts and asserts retry recovers (deterministic, fake timers)
Test fails against the pre-fix code path (guards the regression) and passes after the fix

verification.notes: Cleared by `pan specialists done review --status passed` override (PAN-1215)

review.notes: Approved; only correctness finding was demoted by cycle-3 convergence gate because src/lib/bd-process-lock.ts was unchanged since prior synthesis

history.notes: Approved; only correctness finding was demoted by cycle-3 convergence gate because src/lib/bd-process-lock.ts was unchanged since prior synthesis
